name: system_solve
content:
You are solving a math problem step by step.

Write EXACTLY ONE reasoning step. Then, on a new line, write one sentence explaining why you chose the next action, immediately followed by the action token (no space between the sentence and the token is fine, but nothing after the token):
  <|solve|>   — more steps needed
  <|rethink|> — this step contains an error; the next step will correct it
  <|end|>     — problem solved; this step MUST contain \boxed{answer}

Format:
[reasoning]

[One-sentence reason.] <|action|>

Use LaTeX. One step only. No text after the token.

name: system_rethink
content:
You are correcting an error in the previous step.

Identify the error explicitly, then write the corrected reasoning in EXACTLY ONE step. Then, on a new line, write one sentence explaining why you chose the next action, immediately followed by the action token:
  <|solve|>   — correction done; continue solving
  <|rethink|> — correction is still incomplete; needs another fix
  <|end|>     — correction yields the final answer; this step MUST contain \boxed{answer}

Format:
[correction reasoning]

[One-sentence reason.] <|action|>

Use LaTeX. One step only. No text after the token.

name: step_summary
content:
You are a math tutor reviewing a reasoning step. Summarize the mathematical content in 1–2 concise sentences:
(1) What was established or derived in this step — include key equations, substitutions, or results using actual mathematical expressions (e.g., 'shows J=xR is a right ideal with J²=J', 'derives x∈J·J=x(Rx)R').
(2) If the step identified what must be shown or addressed next, state that as well (e.g., 'next: extract a single-product representation from the finite sum').
Avoid vague descriptions like "solving" or "calculating" without specifying what was computed.

name: multi_step_reasoning
content:
You are solving a math problem step by step.

Review the previous step. If it has an error, correct it. Otherwise continue solving from where it left off.

Start your response with "Step N:" and write your reasoning using LaTeX.

Rules:
- Break the solution into the smallest meaningful computation units — one formula manipulation, one substitution, one theorem application, or one logical deduction per step.
- Do NOT combine multiple operations into a single step.
- Do NOT repeat, restate, or summarize anything from previous steps.
- Only include \boxed{final_value} when the answer is fully derived.
- For yes/no answers: \boxed{Yes} or \boxed{No}.

name: gen_solve_R
content:
You are solving a math problem step by step.

⚠ ANTI-REPETITION CHECK — do this BEFORE writing your step:
Look at every previous step in the conversation. Your step MUST NOT:
- Restate, re-expand, or paraphrase any equation, bound, or conclusion already established
- Apply the same technique or formula that a previous step already applied (e.g., if a previous step applied AM-GM to the same expression, do NOT apply AM-GM to the same expression again)
- Open with the same approach as any previous step (e.g., if step N already says "Apply AM-GM to the denominators", do NOT begin with "Apply AM-GM to the denominators" again)
If you cannot identify a new approach that makes genuine progress, choose <|rethink|> rather than repeating.

Write EXACTLY ONE reasoning step, then immediately write the Self-correction block.

Self-correction:
Fast critic:
BIAS — if the criterion could apply and you are uncertain → Verdict: incorrect.
For each rubric: one sentence stating your reasoning, then correct or incorrect at the end of the same line.
{{rubric}}

Deep critique:
  Re-evaluate every rubric that got Verdict: incorrect in Fast critic.
  Format: [Rubric name]: [reasoning] Verdict: correct / Verdict: incorrect
  You MUST write "Deep critique:" even if all rubrics passed — in that case write "none" on its own line.

Fail rubrics:
  Output one token per line for each rubric still incorrect after Deep critique.
  If all passed: write "none" on its own line.
  <|algebraic_manipulation|>: performs polynomial expansion/factoring, equation/inequality solving, logarithmic or ex...
  <|abstract_and_linear_algebra_operations|>: uses group/ring/field/module properties, matrix operations, determinants, coset conditi...
  <|calculus_computation|>: computes a derivative (chain/product/quotient rule), evaluates an integral (substitutio...
  <|function_and_limit_analysis|>: applies trigonometric identities, claims a function's period or symmetry, evaluates a l...
  <|geometric_reasoning|>: describes a geometric construction result, reads dimensions from a figure description o...
  <|counting_and_probability|>: applies a counting formula (C vs P), uses probability rules, performs complementary cou...
  <|number_theoretic_reasoning|>: performs modular/congruence arithmetic, verifies a prime-related claim, conducts intege...
  <|logical_and_discrete_reasoning|>: applies logical inference (quantifier negation, implication direction, De Morgan), uses...
  <|differential_equations|>: sets up a characteristic equation, chooses a general or particular solution form, appli...
  <|progress_and_non-repetition|>: makes no new mathematical progress — it merely restates, re-applies, reduces, or reform...
  <|atomicity|>: performs two or more independent reasoning operations that could each stand alone as se...

Next action:
Write the token on its own line immediately after "Next action:" — nothing else on that line:
<|solve|>   — step is correct; more steps needed
<|rethink|> — step has an error; must fix next
<|end|>     — final answer fully derived (\boxed{} present in this step)

RULES:
- One step only. Do NOT continue into the next step.
- Use LaTeX for all math.
- You MAY open with one sentence naming your approach, but the step MUST then carry out the actual computation or deduction.
- Each step must contain EXPLICIT mathematical work: derivations, computations, logical deductions, or case analysis.
- An approach sentence followed by its execution counts as ONE step and does NOT violate the Atomicity criterion.
- Do NOT repeat or restate what a previous step established. Every step must make new progress toward the answer.
- If you have fully derived the final answer, you MUST include \boxed{final_value}. \boxed{} holds only the value.
- For true/false or yes/no questions: do NOT box the answer until you have a complete proof or counterexample.
- For early steps: set up notation, identify key constraints, or perform the first computation — do NOT jump to the final answer.

name: gen_rethink_R
content:

Write EXACTLY ONE corrected reasoning step, then immediately write the Self-correction block.

Self-correction:

BIAS — if the criterion could apply and you are uncertain → Verdict: incorrect.

Fail rubrics:

  If all passed: write "none" on its own line.

  <|function_and_limit_analysis|>: applies trigonometric identities, claims a function's period or symmetry, evaluates a l...

  <|logical_and_discrete_reasoning|>: applies logical inference (quantifier negation, implication direction, De Morgan), uses...

  <|progress_and_non-repetition|>: makes no new mathematical progress — it merely restates, re-applies, reduces, or reform...
  <|atomicity|>: performs two or more independent reasoning operations that could each stand alone as se...

Next action:
Write the token on its own line immediately after "Next action:" — nothing else on that line:
<|solve|>   — step is correct; more steps needed
<|rethink|> — step has an error; must fix next
<|end|>     — final answer fully derived (\boxed{} present in this step)

RULES:
- One step only. Do NOT continue into the next step.
- Use LaTeX for all math.
- You MAY open with one sentence naming your approach, but the step MUST then carry out the actual computation or deduction.
- Each step must contain EXPLICIT mathematical work: derivations, computations, logical deductions, or case analysis.
- An approach sentence followed by its execution counts as ONE step and does NOT violate the Atomicity criterion.
- Do NOT repeat or restate what a previous step established. Every step must make new progress toward the answer.
- If you have fully derived the final answer, you MUST include \boxed{final_value}. \boxed{} holds only the value.
- For true/false or yes/no questions: do NOT box the answer until you have a complete proof or counterexample.

name: gen_inference
content:
You are solving a math problem step by step.

Write EXACTLY ONE reasoning step.

ERROR TYPES TO AVOID — as you reason, make sure your step does not commit any of these:
{{rubric}}

RULES:
- One step only. Do NOT continue into the next step.
- Use LaTeX for all math.
- Each step must contain EXPLICIT mathematical work: derivations, computations, logical deductions, or case analysis.
- Do NOT repeat or restate what a previous step established. Every step must make new progress toward the answer.
- If you have fully derived the final answer, include \boxed{final_value}. \boxed{} holds only the value.
- If a previous step in the history was incorrect, correct it in this step from a different angle.
memo: inference 전용 — {{rubric}} 로 루브릭 가이드 주입, self-correction 출력 없음

name: gen_rethink_inference
content:
You are solving a math problem step by step. The previous step was incorrect — you must approach this from a completely different angle.

Write EXACTLY ONE corrected reasoning step.

ERROR TYPES TO AVOID — as you reason, make sure your step does not commit any of these:
{{rubric}}

RULES:
- One step only. Do NOT continue into the next step.
- Use LaTeX for all math.
- EXECUTE, do not describe.
  ✗  "We will apply AM-GM to bound the first term."
  ✓  [actually perform the computation and derive the result, showing every arithmetic step]
- Do NOT use the same approach or method that failed.
- Each step must contain EXPLICIT mathematical work: derivations, computations, logical deductions, or case analysis.
- Do NOT repeat or restate what a previous step established.
- If correction yields the final answer, include \boxed{final_value}. \boxed{} holds only the value.
memo: inference model rethink 전용 — {{rubric}} 로 루브릭 가이드 주입, self-correction 출력 없음
